top_findings.title: SQLite store is in-memory (`:memory:`) over a pooled sql.DB — non-persistent and unsafe under concurrency
top_findings.evidence: main.go:15 NewBookStore(":memory:"); database.go:19-44 opens a database/sql pool with no SetMaxOpenConns(1). With mattn/go-sqlite3 each pooled connection to :memory: is a SEPARATE database, so under concurrent requests a query can land on a connection whose books table/data is absent (no such table: books / lost writes). Data is also lost on every restart. Tests pass only because they run sequentially on one reused connection.
top_findings.suggestion: Use a file-backed DSN (e.g. books.db) or file::memory:?cache=shared, and call db.SetMaxOpenConns(1) for in-memory use to keep all access on one connection.

tracker_note: file-run-issues v2.0 aggregates into assessment.json only; it does not create beads/GitHub issues (see skill Constraints). tracker=beads had no effect.
